title: Tier-Eval v2 Test Manifest Entry
description: Per-test metadata declared in a manifest header inside each test file under host/test/__tests__/tier-eval/. See TIER-EVAL-V2-SPRINT-PLAN.md §3 (Sprint 0.3 + 0.4) and the source strategy doc §4. Capability axes drive cross-tier reporting; suite_layer determines whether the test counts toward smoke (A), the core stratification matrix (B), the diagnostic deep-dive (C), or the frontier/stress suite (D).

test_id.description: Stable test identifier. Convention: matches the basename of the test file (e.g. `expression-eval` for `expression-eval.test.js`).

test_version.description: Exact prompt/verifier version. Bump when the prompt or oracle changes; older runs keep their original test_version on the registry row.

primary_axis.description: Single dominant capability axis from strategy doc §4. Drives axis-scorecard reporting.

secondary_axes.description: Up to two additional axes this test exercises. Optional; do not duplicate primary_axis.

suite_layer.description: Strategy doc §3: A = smoke/harness-health, B = core stratification, C = diagnostic deep-dive, D = frontier/stress. Layer-A failures must not be aggregated into model pass-rate comparisons.

difficulty_band.description: Coarse difficulty signal. `frontier` is reserved for Layer-D stress tests and tests expected to floor on smaller tiers.

oracle_type.description: How pass/fail is determined. Must match the `oracle_type` recorded on every run-registry row produced by this test.

known_confounds.description: Free-form list of known confounds (e.g. `context_pressure_high`, `tool_shape_variance`, `repo_size_dependent`). Surfaced in the discrimination matrix so a noisy result can be interpreted.

keep_drop_rule.description: One-sentence rule for when this test should leave the core matrix. Examples: `Drop if pass_rate >= 0.95 across all three tiers in confirmatory n>=40 runs.`, `Never drop — axis-critical for spec_precision.`, `Move to Layer D if pass_rate <= 0.10 across all three tiers in confirmatory n>=40 runs.`

expected_tier_signature.description: Pre-registered hypothesis about how this test should behave across tiers. Used to flag surprises (e.g. a test marked `monotonic_improving` that ends up `ceiling` warrants investigation).

introduced_in.description: Harness version or date when this test was introduced. Helps interpret historical-bucketing decisions.

notes.description: Free-form notes (e.g. `originally W4-A failure-mode probe; retained because expression-eval-style edge density is axis-critical for spec_precision`).
